title_en: Scholarly Reception and the Tradition of Commentary
content_en:
Al-Furuq was recognized as a major scholarly achievement from its completion and has remained a foundational text in Maliki legal education and scholarship. Its most important early engagement came from Ibn al-Shatt (d. 723 AH), a Maliki jurist from Tlemcen in the Maghreb, who produced a detailed critical commentary titled Idrar al-Shuruq ala Anwa al-Furuq. Ibn al-Shatt's commentary is remarkable for its intellectual independence: rather than simply explaining or defending al-Qarafi's distinctions, he subjects them to rigorous critical examination, identifies cases where he believes al-Qarafi's analysis is mistaken, and proposes corrections. This critical dialogue was incorporated into the standard editions of Al-Furuq, giving readers access to both al-Qarafi's original argument and his most important critic in a single text.

The Ottoman period saw continued engagement with Al-Furuq as a reference work for Maliki jurisprudence. In the North African and Egyptian scholarly tradition, knowledge of al-Qarafi's distinctions was considered part of the formation of a properly educated Maliki jurist. The work is cited extensively in later Maliki encyclopedias and commentaries, particularly in the discussions of commercial transactions and procedure where al-Qarafi's analysis was felt to be most incisive.

Modern scholarship has found Al-Furuq exceptionally rich for several reasons. Contemporary scholars of Islamic law have recognized al-Qarafi's work as an important early contribution to the theory of legal principles (al-qawa'id al-fiqhiyyah), a genre that has attracted enormous interest in the context of Islamic legal codification and reform. His treatment of fatwa versus qada' has been extensively discussed in scholarship on Islamic courts, legal pluralism, and the role of the mufti in Muslim societies. His observations about custom have been cited in support of theories of contextual legal reasoning.

The Egyptian jurist Muhammad Ahmad Siraj produced a valuable modern study of al-Qarafi's legal methodology that situates Al-Furuq within his broader intellectual project. The work has also been discussed extensively in Western academic scholarship on Islamic law, appearing in studies by Wael Hallaq, Sherman Jackson, and others who have found in al-Qarafi a sophisticated and analytically rigorous interlocutor for comparative legal theory.
content_id:
Al-Furuq diakui sebagai pencapaian ilmiah besar sejak penyelesaiannya dan tetap menjadi teks fondasi dalam pendidikan dan beasiswa hukum Maliki. Keterlibatan paling penting pada masa awal datang dari Ibn al-Syath (wafat 723 H), seorang ahli fiqh Maliki dari Tlemcen di Maghrib, yang menghasilkan komentar kritis terperinci berjudul Idrar al-Syuruq ala Anwa al-Furuq. Komentar Ibn al-Syath luar biasa karena kemandirian intelektualnya: alih-alih sekadar menjelaskan atau membela perbedaan-perbedaan al-Qarafi, beliau mengkaji mereka dengan pemeriksaan kritis yang ketat, mengidentifikasi kasus-kasus di mana menurutnya analisis al-Qarafi keliru, dan mengusulkan koreksi. Dialog kritis ini dimasukkan ke dalam edisi-edisi standar Al-Furuq, memberikan para pembaca akses ke argumen asli al-Qarafi dan kritikusnya yang paling penting dalam satu teks.

Periode Ottoman menyaksikan keterlibatan berkelanjutan dengan Al-Furuq sebagai karya referensi untuk fiqh Maliki. Dalam tradisi keilmuan Afrika Utara dan Mesir, pengetahuan tentang perbedaan-perbedaan al-Qarafi dianggap sebagai bagian dari pembentukan seorang ahli fiqh Maliki yang terdidik dengan baik. Karya ini dikutip secara ekstensif dalam ensiklopedia dan komentar Maliki yang lebih belakangan, khususnya dalam pembahasan transaksi komersial dan prosedur di mana analisis al-Qarafi dianggap paling tajam.

Beasiswa modern telah menemukan Al-Furuq sangat kaya untuk beberapa alasan. Para sarjana kontemporer hukum Islam telah mengakui karya al-Qarafi sebagai kontribusi awal yang penting bagi teori prinsip-prinsip hukum (al-qawa'id al-fiqhiyyah), sebuah genre yang telah menarik minat besar dalam konteks kodifikasi dan reformasi hukum Islam. Perlakuannya terhadap fatwa versus qadha' telah dibahas secara ekstensif dalam beasiswa tentang pengadilan Islam, pluralisme hukum, dan peran mufti dalam masyarakat Muslim. Pengamatannya tentang adat telah dikutip dalam mendukung teori-teori penalaran hukum kontekstual.

Ahli fiqh Mesir Muhammad Ahmad Siraj menghasilkan sebuah studi modern yang berharga tentang metodologi hukum al-Qarafi yang menempatkan Al-Furuq dalam proyek intelektualnya yang lebih luas. Karya ini juga telah dibahas secara ekstensif dalam beasiswa akademis Barat tentang hukum Islam, muncul dalam studi-studi oleh Wael Hallaq, Sherman Jackson, dan lain-lain yang menemukan al-Qarafi sebagai lawan bicara yang canggih dan analitis ketat untuk teori hukum komparatif.

Warisan Al-Furuq dalam tradisi keilmuan Islam melampaui relevansinya bagi praktisi fiqh Maliki. Ini adalah contoh terbaik dari apa yang disebut oleh para sarjana modern sebagai 'ilmu kaidah fiqhiyyah' — suatu disiplin yang berusaha mengidentifikasi prinsip-prinsip mendasar yang memberikan koherensi pada hukum Islam di berbagai domain. Kontribusi al-Qarafi terhadap disiplin ini, yang dikristalkan dalam Al-Furuq, merupakan karya abadi yang terus membentuk cara ulama berpikir tentang hubungan antara prinsip dan kasus, antara aturan umum dan aplikasi spesifiknya.
content_ar: Al-Furuq was recognized as a major العلمي achievement from its completion and has remained a foundational text in المالكي legal education and scholarship. Its most المهم early engagement came from Ibn al-Shatt (d. 723 AH), a المالكي jurist from Tlemcen in the Maghreb, who produced a detailed البالغ الأهمية commentary titled Idrar al-Shuruq ala Anwa al-Furuq. Ibn al-Shatt's commentary is remarkable for its intellectual independence: rather than simply explaining or defending al-Qarafi's distinctions, he subjects them to rigorous البالغ الأهمية examination, identifies cases where he believes al-Qarafi's analysis is mistaken, and proposes corrections. This البالغ الأهمية dialogue was incorporated into the standard editions of Al-Furuq, giving readers access to both al-Qarafi's original argument and his most المهم critic in a single text. The Ottoman period saw continued engagement with Al-Furuq as a reference work for المالكي jurisprudence. In the North African and Egyptian العلمي التراث, العلم of al-Qarafi's distinctions was considered part of the formation of a properly educated المالكي jurist. The work is cited extensively in later المالكي encyclopedias and commentaries, particularly in the discussions of commercial transactions and procedure where al-Qarafi's analysis was felt to be most incisive. Modern scholarship has found Al-Furuq exceptionally rich for several reasons. Contemporary العلماء of Islamic law have recognized al-Qarafi's work as an المهم early contribution to the theory of legal principles (al-qawa'id al-fiqhiyyah), a genre that has attracted enormous interest in the context of Islamic legal codification and reform. His treatment of الفتوى versus القضاء' has been extensively discussed in scholarship on Islamic courts, legal pluralism, and the role of the mufti in مسلم societies. His observations about custom have been cited in support of theories of contextual legal reasoning. The Egyptian jurist محمد ﷺ أحمد Siraj produced a valuable modern study of al-Qarafi's legal methodology that situates Al-Furuq within his broader intellectual project. The work has أيضاً been discussed extensively in Western الأكاديمي scholarship on Islamic law, appearing in studies by Wael Hallaq, Sherman Jackson, and others who have found in al-Qarafi a sophisticated and analytically rigorous interlocutor for comparative legal theory.
